nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q13.alt: Question 13. What does the phrase “set out” mean as it is used in paragraph 6 of the article? Choices: A: grabbed her suitcase B: began her journey C: accepted work D: started writing

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q14.alt: Question 14. Read this sentence from paragraph 6. Traveling east across the Atlantic, Nellie took just one bag in order to move quickly. How is this detail important to paragraph 1? Choices: A: It shows how she is the same as the character in the book. B: It shows one way to help her reach her goal. C: It shows a young reporter exploring the world in real life. D: It shows that a young woman taking a trip alone is unusual.

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q15.alt: Question 15. How does the author organize the information in paragraphs 6 and 7? Choices: A: by listing events in the order they happened B: by comparing and contrasting the places Nellie visited C: by showing what caused Nellie to want to take the trip D: by stating how the problem of traveling so far was solved

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q16.alt: Question 16. What does the word “journalism” mean as it is used in paragraph 9? Choices: A: writing for newspapers B: traveling for women C: finding adventures D: discovering opinions

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q17.alt: Question 17. Which detail from the article does the map support? Choices: A: “Start the man, and I’ll start the same day . . .” (paragraph 2) B: “She reached New York in 4½ days.” (paragraph 7) C: “She met her challenge!” (paragraph 8) D: “She reported to her readers what she saw, thought, and felt . . .” (paragraph 9)

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q18.alt: Question 18. Which sentence best states the main idea of “Around the World”? Choices: A: Traveling around the world in a short period of time is a difficult goal. B: It was unusual for a woman to travel in the past. C: Geography is an important subject to study. D: A woman showed she can do anything.

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q19.alt: Question 19. What does the word “demand” mean as it is used in paragraphs 2 and 21? Choices: A: to correct B: to look for C: to work on D: to insist

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q20.alt: Question 20. What does the reader know in paragraphs 6 through 8 that the tigers do not know? Choices: A: King Tiger wants to take food from the Island of Borneo. B: The Island of Borneo has no tiger king. C: Mouse Deer is afraid of King Tiger’s whisker. D: The whisker from Mouse Deer is not from a tiger.

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q21.alt: Question 21. Paragraph 15 supports a theme of the story by showing that Mouse Deer Choices: A: honors the tigers B: is clever and brave C: is happy to be telling lies D: finds his king

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q22.alt: Question 22. In paragraph 17, the phrase “pranced off” shows that Mouse Deer feels Choices: A: eager to run far away from the tigers B: satisfied with how things went with the tigers C: worried that the tigers will return with their king D: surprised to see that the tigers could swim

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q23.alt: Question 23. Which sentence best describes how Mouse Deer causes the event in paragraph 21? Choices: A: He sees three tigers looking at him. B: He carries King Tiger’s whisker in his mouth. C: He asks Porcupine for one of his quills. D: He waits so the tigers will think he went far.

nysed-ela-2019-g4-mc-q24.alt: Question 24. What do the details in paragraph 21 suggest about King Tiger? Choices: A: He wants to avoid showing that he is scared. B: He believes there is more food on a different island. C: He knows that it is important to plan ahead. D: He thinks he is the greatest tiger in the world.
